heading: Post office conducted without authority
label: 1729 Post office conducted without authority
text: Whoever, without authority from the Postal Service, sets up or professes to keep any office or place of business bearing the sign, name, or title of post office, shall be fined under this title.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 302 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 17935 Stat. 1123Minor verbal changes were made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–3221970— substituted “Postal Service” for “Postmaster General”.Pub. L. 91–375

Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentAmendment by effective within 1 year after , on date established therefor by Board of Governors of United States Postal Service and published by it in Federal Register, see , set out as an Effective Date note preceding , Postal Service.Pub.
